title: Why Ice Melts
intro_hook: Garmi mein glass ke bahar paani kahan se aata hai aur barf kyun pighalti hai? Kabhi socha hai?
big_idea: Heat is energy. When ice (solid) gets heat from the surroundings, its particles move faster and break free, turning into water (liquid).

cause_process_result.cause: Heat from the room transfers to the cold ice.
cause_process_result.process: The water molecules in ice start vibrating faster because of the heat energy.
cause_process_result.result: The bonds holding them together break, and the solid ice turns into liquid water.

indian_analogies: Jaise bheed mein log shant khade hote hain (solid), aur phir DJ bajne par sab dance karne lagte hain aur idhar-udhar ghoomne lagte hain (liquid) — heat wahi DJ hai!

misconceptions.belief: Ice leaks coldness into the water.
misconceptions.probe: Kya sach mein coldness leak hoti hai, ya heat andar jaati hai?

practice_problems.q: Agar hum barf ko blanket mein lapet dein, toh kya wo jaldi pighlegi ya der se?
practice_problems.hint: Blanket heat ko rokta hai, jaise humein thand se bachata hai.

question_flow.q: When ice is kept in a warm room, where does the heat move?
Do we add coldness to the room, or does heat enter the ice?
What changes in the particles when solid ice becomes liquid water?
Explain ice melting to a younger student in two or three lines.

teach_back_prompt: Explain why ice melts using heat, particles, and change of state.
